items.title: More Python parallel stuff with joblib-modal
items.author: probabl
items.content:
One of the probabl colleagues has been working on an integration between joblib (which scikit-learn uses for parallel processing) and Modal (a cloud-provider known for it's likeable configuration for compute resources in Python). In this livestream we will explore a very early version and show you what's possible now and what is different. 

Website: https://probabl.ai/
Discord: https://discord.probabl.ai/
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/probabl
Twitter: https://x.com/probabl_ai

We also host a podcast called Sample Space, which you can find on your favourite podcast player. All the links can be found here:
https://rss.com/podcasts/sample-space/

If you're keen to see more videos like this, you can follow us over at  @probabl_ai.

items.title: Dumb models can be very smart
items.author: probabl
items.content:
Dummy models are models that really just make a prediction without learning any mayor patterns from your dataset. But what makes them useful is that they can be compared to other models. If your trained system cannot outperform a dummy model then you've got a signal to dive deeper.

00:00 Just metrics
04:11 Toward dummy models 
07:35 Regression as well

items.title: ModernBert: time for a new BERT
items.author: probabl
items.content:
During the holiday season there has been a bit of buzz around a new family of BERT models called ModernBert. We figured we might explore some of these models at the start of this year to see of these models might also be useful for scikit-learn pipelines.

items.title: What the official scikit-learn certification looks like
items.author: probabl
items.content:
We got a lot of questions about our certification program. Some of these questions weren't so much on the material, but more on the medium and interface of the actual exam. 

That why we made this small recording. It shows what you can expect as we go through a few questions in a mock exam.

00:00 Intro and setup
01:56 Starting the exam
03:19 First questions
05:41 Programming exercise
09:47 Final review at the end

To learn more about the official scikit-learn certification program go here: 
https://certification.probabl.ai/

items.title: When precision equals recall
items.author: probabl
items.content:
Precision can actually be equal to recall. For balanced datasets it can even be pretty common! But understanding when this happens may also help you understand both metrics a bit more. 

00:00 Introduction 
00:32 Experiment setup 
03:45 Code
06:45 Why? 
09:30 Math proof via sympy

Appendix with notebooks:
https://github.com/probabl-ai/youtube-appendix/tree/main/16-metrics

items.title: Ask me Anything[tm] and a certification review!
items.author: probabl
items.content:
We are nearing the holiday season, which is why this livestream is a bit different. Vincent will try to answer your questions (about ML) and will also review some pointers for those that are interested in taking the certification exams during the holiday. We will mainly focus on the associate level, but we will discuss what areas of the library to study!
